title: File Motions
subtitle: gg, G, {n}G — jump anywhere in the file.
summary: gg goes to line 1. G goes to the last line. {n}G or :{n} goes to line n.

blocks.text: Three jumps cover most navigation across a file: top, bottom, and a specific line number.
{key:5}{key:0}{key:G} goes to line 50. So does {key::}{key:5}{key:0}{key:Enter}. Pick whichever your fingers reach faster.

blocks.title: These Are All "Big" Jumps
blocks.text:
Vim distinguishes *small* movements ({key:h}, {key:j}, {key:w}) from *big* jumps ({key:gg}, {key:G}, search, {key:%}, mark-jumps, {key:`}). Big jumps push the *previous* cursor position onto the **jump list** before moving.

That means after {key:G}-then-edit-then-{key:gg}, you can press {key:Ctrl-O} to jump *back* to where you came from. Try it — it changes how you navigate.

Deep dive: [`marks.jumplist`](#marks.jumplist).

{key:g}{key:g} and {key:G} are also operands. {key:d}{key:G} deletes from here to end of file (linewise). {key:y}{key:g}{key:g} yanks from here to top. {key:=}{key:g}{key:g} re-indents from cursor to top. Consistency, again.
